text.title: One-Way-Door Flagging Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `One-Way-Door Flagging Protocol` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every ADR MUST carry `decision_class: one-way-door | two-way-door` in the header. Missing field = ADR INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Implicit reversibility lives in tribal knowledge; explicit field forces calibration.

rule.statement: One-way-door decisions MUST sit ≥ 48h between proposal and approval. Bypass requires a documented incident exemption.
rule.rationale: Cooldowns reduce regretted decisions in field studies.

rule.statement: One-way-door ADRs MUST list a named devil's-advocate reviewer separate from the author.
rule.rationale: Author-only review misses author blind spots.

rule.statement: One-way-door ADRs MUST include a documented rollback estimate (cost, time, blast-radius). 'Not reversible' counts but MUST be stated.
rule.rationale: Rollback estimate forces the author to see the future cost of regret.

rule.statement: Two-way doors take the standard PR path — no cooldown, no devil's advocate. The protocol exists to free velocity, not throttle it.
rule.rationale: Equal review for unequal stakes is the failure mode the protocol prevents.

rule.statement: Every quarter, sample ≥ 5 decisions per class and review classification correctness; recalibrate criteria from drift.
rule.rationale: Without calibration, the field becomes theatre.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
